search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae muscle sprains strains Issue 2 history red flags investigations referral
site:who.int ICRC basic emergency care extremity trauma sprain
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic sprain grade or fracture exclusion
automatic RICE, analgesia, imaging, splint, rehabilitation or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow remains presentation documentation and does not establish a sprain grade or exclude fracture.
All tests, treatments, medicines and referrals remain clinician-selected.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with sprain- or strain-compatible injuries suitable for assessment; open injury, major trauma, neurovascular compromise, infection and fracture concern require distinct pathways.
setting_applicability: DHA telehealth evidence is direct for Dubai virtual care and partial for in-person emergency documentation.
UAE_applicability: UAE-direct DHA evidence applies in licensed Dubai telehealth; facility imaging, orthopaedic, medication and emergency pathways govern elsewhere.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 effective 2024-04-21 with revision due 2029-02-21 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Issue 2 remains the current DHA telehealth version.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA sprain/strain history section supports onset, injury context, severity, functional impact, associated symptoms and relevant negatives for a sprain-compatible presentation without asserting a diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag and investigation section supports clinician-recorded risk context, manual focused examination, injury severity, appearance and already performed or reviewed imaging without selecting a fracture, test or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA management and referral section supports recording a clinician-decided plan, specialist discussion and arranged follow-up only when those actions occurred; no management is preselected.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact joint, side, mechanism, deformity, neurovascular findings, weight bearing, diagnosis, grade, treatment, medicine, procedure and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
